title: Today's AI Brief: 100 things we announced at I/O 2026
summary: Google's I/O 2026 announcements and Melbourne's AI innovation

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI brief. This week, Google dropped a massive bundle of announcements at their I/O 2026 event. I'm talking 100 things - yes, you read that right, 100! It's like they decided to make a big ol' laundry list of all the cool AI stuff they've been working on.

So, let's dive into a few of the interesting bits. Google announced a bunch of new AI features and tools, which is pretty standard for them, but the scale here is massive. I am looking at the list and it's everything from improvements to their language models to new ways of interacting with data. My read is that they're trying to make AI more accessible and useful for everyone.

Also making waves is Melbourne's AI and Data Center, which is apparently becoming a hub for research innovation. According to IEEE Spectrum, Melbourne's reputation as a global events city is translating into a flywheel for AI and data research. It's interesting to see how different cities are positioning themselves for AI growth.

Another item that caught my eye is the focus on safety, trust, and distribution in AI. As AI becomes more pervasive, these issues are becoming more pressing. It's a complex problem, but it's great to see companies and researchers working on it.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
